title: quercus robur subvar. astrotricha
intro: Quercus robur subvar. astrotricha, also known as the Astrotricha English oak, is a subvariety of the pedunculate oak tree (Quercus robur) native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive, narrow, and pointed leaf lobes. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions, such as diarrhea, fever, and skin issues. The health benefits of Quercus robur subvar. astrotricha include its ability to support digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. The bark and leaves can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties and provide a more comprehensive treatment.
